portation. Then, as a creditor jeers

further rubbing it in on those legis-
lators who sat there and hypocritical-

marks, the railroad lobbyist said:


This might hit some of you MEM
bers of the Legislature, but then you
know we're all trying to get fair
BILL


"Stil, I'm willing to furnish you

So"


Many of the committee laughed at
these remarks and, growing still
holder, Mr.. Cotton ended with the
following threat:


I'VE want to keep out of politics,
but In such an event we would be
vitality interested in the personnel
of commission in this state"


The outspoken admissions of rail-
road bribery by means of free passes
to the legislature were tentatively
admitted by the members of the joint
committee, not one of whom arose
to protest against such disgraceful
conditions. 1t was in the corridor
late that Representative Crane met
Mr.. Cotton and made the remark
quoted above. to which Mr.. Cotton
made no reply.
